mnemonics.title: UNIX PERMISSIONS MNEMONIC
mnemonics.content: READ = 4, WRITE = 2, EXECUTE = 1. REMEMBER: R-W-X IN ALPHABETICAL ORDER = 4-2-1. ALSO THINK 'R' FOR READING (YOU CAN '4' GET INFO), 'W' FOR WRITING (YOU '2' WORK WITH IT), 'X' FOR EXECUTING (E'X'ECUTABLE FILES NEED '1' PERMISSION).

mnemonics.title: ACL VS CAPABILITY LIST
mnemonics.content: ACL = A FILE-CENTRIC APPROACH (A FOR 'A FILE'), CAPABILITY = USER-CENTRIC APPROACH. REMEMBER: 'ACL = ACCESS CONTROL ON LIST OF FILE' AND 'CAPABILITY = CARD THAT USER CARRIES'.

mnemonics.title: TYPES OF PROTECTION
mnemonics.content: COMPLETE = OWNER CONTROLS EVERYTHING, LIMITED = OWNER CONTROLS TYPES, PASSWORD = SECRET CODE NEEDED. REMEMBER: 'C-L-P' - C FOR COMPLETE, L FOR LIMITED, P FOR PASSWORD.

acronyms.usage: A list associated with each file containing user names and their access rights. Unix uses this with chmod.
Protection model where resource owners can discretionarily grant/revoke access to others. Unix permissions are DAC.
Strict model where system enforces access based on security labels. Used in military/government systems.
Access rights are assigned to roles, users are assigned to roles. Common in organizations.
Unix command that determines default permissions by masking out certain bits when creating files.

visualTips.visualization: DRAW A TABLE: ROWS = SUBJECTS (Users/Processes), COLUMNS = OBJECTS (Files). CELLS CONTAIN WHAT OPERATIONS EACH SUBJECT CAN PERFORM ON EACH OBJECT.
THINK OF 3 GROUPS OF 3 BITS: [OWNER][GROUP][OTHERS]. EACH GROUP: [READ(4)][WRITE(2)][EXECUTE(1)]. VISUALIZE AS 3 SETS OF TRIANGLES FOR EACH GROUP.
DRAW TWO APPROACHES: 1) FILE-CENTRIC: FILE HAS LIST OF WHO CAN ACCESS IT. 2) USER-CENTRIC: USER HAS LIST OF WHAT THEY CAN ACCESS.

analogies.analogy: PROTECTION DOMAIN IS LIKE A SECURITY CLEARANCE LEVEL. JUST AS SOMEONE WITH 'SECRET' CLEARANCE CANNOT ACCESS 'TOP SECRET' AREAS, A PROCESS IN ONE DOMAIN CANNOT ACCESS OBJECTS IN ANOTHER DOMAIN WITHOUT PROPER RIGHTS.
ACL IS LIKE A GUEST LIST AT A CLUB (THE FILE DECIDES WHO ENTERS). CAPABILITY LIST IS LIKE AN ID CARD YOU CARRY (SHOWS WHAT YOU ARE ALLOWED TO DO).
IT IS LIKE GIVING SOMEONE A KEYS: IF THEY ONLY NEED TO OPEN ONE ROOM, GIVE THEM THAT ONE KEY, NOT THE MASTER KEY TO THE ENTIRE BUILDING.
UNIX PERMISSIONS ARE LIKE A HOTEL KEY CARD SYSTEM: OWNER HAS FULL ACCESS (MASTER KEY), GROUP MEMBERS HAVE SHARED ACCESS, OTHERS HAVE LIMITED ACCESS.
